title: General Knowledge
description: A broad mix of science, history, art, and pop culture questions to challenge every trivia enthusiast.

questions.explanation: Leonardo da Vinci painted the Mona Lisa between approximately 1503 and 1519. It now hangs in the Louvre Museum in Paris, France.

questions.text: How many sides does a hexagon have?
questions.explanation: A hexagon has six sides and six angles. The word comes from the Greek 'hex' (six) and 'gonia' (corner). Honeycombs are a natural hexagonal structure.

questions.text: What is the chemical symbol for gold?
questions.explanation: Gold's symbol Au comes from the Latin word 'aurum'. It is element 79 on the periodic table and has been valued by civilizations for thousands of years.

questions.text: Which planet is closest to the Sun?
questions.explanation: Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun, orbiting at an average distance of about 58 million km. Despite this, Venus is actually hotter due to its thick atmosphere.

questions.text: In what year did World War II end?
questions.explanation: World War II ended in 1945: Germany surrendered on May 8 (V-E Day) and Japan surrendered on September 2 (V-J Day) following the atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki.

questions.text: What is the largest ocean on Earth?
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean is the largest and deepest ocean on Earth, covering more than 30% of the planet's surface — larger than all of Earth's landmasses combined.

questions.text: Who wrote the play 'Romeo and Juliet'?
questions.explanation: William Shakespeare wrote Romeo and Juliet around 1594–1596. It is one of his most famous tragedies and remains one of the most performed plays in history.

questions.text: How many bones are in the adult human body?
questions.explanation: An adult human body has 206 bones. Babies are born with around 270–300 bones, many of which fuse together during childhood and adolescence.

questions.text: Which country has the world's largest population?
questions.explanation: India surpassed China as the world's most populous country in 2023, with a population of over 1.4 billion people.

questions.text: What gas do plants absorb from the atmosphere during photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: Plants absorb carbon dioxide (CO₂) and water, using sunlight energy to convert them into glucose and oxygen via photosynthesis. This process is fundamental to life on Earth.
